title: sorbus meridionalis
intro: Sorbus meridionalis, also known as the South European whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the rose family (Rosaceae), native to the Iberian Peninsula and southern France. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of digestive issues and fever, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Sorbus meridionalis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its medicinal properties, and can also be consumed as a raw fruit or used in jams and preserves.
